segments.text: Man, who is born of a woman, is of few days, and full of trouble.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He grows up like a flower, and is cut down. He also flees like a shadow, and doesn’t continue.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Do you open your eyes on such a one, and bring me into judgment with you?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? Not one.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Seeing his days are determined, 
the number of his months is with you, 
and you have appointed his bounds that he can’t pass.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Look away from him, that he may rest, until he accomplishes, as a hireling, his day.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
“For there is hope for a tree if it is cut down, that it will sprout again, 
that the tender branch of it will not cease.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Though its root grows old in the earth, and its stock dies in the ground,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: yet through the scent of water it will bud, and sprout boughs like a plant.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But man dies, and is laid low. Yes, man gives up the spirit, and where is he?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: As the waters fail from the sea, and the river wastes and dries up,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
so man lies down and doesn’t rise. Until the heavens are no more, they will not awake, 
nor be roused out of their sleep.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
“Oh that you would hide me in Sheol,  that you would keep me secret until your wrath is past, 
that you would appoint me a set time and remember me!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
If a man dies, will he live again? I would wait all the days of my warfare, 
until my release should come.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You would call, and I would answer you. You would have a desire for the work of your hands.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But now you count my steps. Don’t you watch over my sin?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My disobedience is sealed up in a bag. You fasten up my iniquity.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “But the mountain falling comes to nothing. The rock is removed out of its place.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
The waters wear the stones. The torrents of it wash away the dust of the earth. 
So you destroy the hope of man.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You forever prevail against him, and he departs. You change his face, and send him away.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: His sons come to honor, and he doesn’t know it. They are brought low, but he doesn’t perceive it of them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But his flesh on him has pain, and his soul within him mourns.
segments.speaker: Unknown
